hook.text: Shale, sandstone, and conglomerate are all made from broken fragments of older rocks. Destruction creates new rock.

buildup.text: Clastic rocks form from weathered fragments, called clasts, that are transported, deposited, and lithified.

discovery.text: Grain size defines them: clay makes shale, sand makes sandstone, and gravel makes conglomerate.

twist.text: The grain size reveals the energy of the environment. Fast rivers carry gravel; still lakes settle only clay.

climax.text: Clastic rocks are storytellers. Their grains reveal ancient rivers, deserts, glaciers, and ocean floors.

punchline.text: Every grain remembers where it traveled.

quiz.question: What determines the type of clastic sedimentary rock?
options: The size of the sediment grains
